title: Explore the Blue Kingdom: An Ocean Adventure

style_guide.art_style: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. The lighting is soft diffused sunlight from the upper left, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows.
style_guide.color_palette: Cerulean blue, aquamarine, sand yellow, deep navy, coral pink, seaweed green, sunlit white, and hints of shimmering pearl.
style_guide.lighting: Soft diffused daylight from the upper left, warm golden highlights, gentle cool shadows.

characters.name: Leo
characters.description: A girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard.

pages.narration: WOW! Look at that endless blue! You are now an ocean explorer. Did you know that the ocean covers over 70% of our entire planet? It holds mysterious worlds deeper than mountains are tall! Are you ready to see what is hiding below the surface?
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. Leo, a girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard, stands on the deck of a research vessel, pointing excitedly at the vast, sun-dappled turquoise ocean. The light is soft diffused sunlight from the upper left, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: Research ship on the open ocean

pages.narration: Let's go deeper! The warm sunlight can't reach this dark place. It's called the Twilight Zone. Look at that! Strange, glowing animals light up the dark, like a shimmering fireworks display. These animals make their own cool light called bioluminescence!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. Leo, a girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard, is visible in the bubble cockpit of a submarine, peering wide-eyed at bioluminescent creatures glowing like fireflies in the dim, deep blue water. The only light comes from the glowing animals and the submarine's faint internal glow, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: Even further down, where the pressure is huge and the water is icy cold! This is the Midnight Zone, where no sunlight ever shines. But life still finds a way! We need a really strong submarine to visit these bizarre creatures. Where should we go next?
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. The strong deep-sea submersible 'Trident', with its lights beaming into the absolute darkness of the Midnight Zone, illuminates an eerie, muddy seafloor. No people are visible. The lights catch a bizarre anglerfish swimming slowly by, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

option_a.label: Sunlight Zone: Swim with Ocean Giants
option_a.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. A massive blue whale breaches the shimmering, sunlit surface of the ocean, surrounded by a pod of dolphins. Warm diffused daylight from the upper left creates golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: Back up we go, to where the sun is warm and bright! This is the Sunlight Zone, and it's full of life because plants called phytoplankton can grow here using photosynthesis. These tiny plants make half of the world's oxygen! Look, here come some playful friends!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. Leo, a girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard, smiles broadly, waving through the submersible bubble at a pod of wild bottlenose dolphins swimming close to the glass in clear, sun-drenched azure water. The lighting is soft diffused sunlight from the upper left, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: Incredible! We just found a colossal animal. Blue whales are the largest creatures to EVER live on Earth, bigger than the biggest dinosaurs! They eat tiny krill and migrate thousands of miles every year. What a giant surprise!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. Leo, a girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard, looks awe-struck in her submersible bubble as a massive Humpback whale breaches the water nearby, a huge splash visible against the golden sunset sky. The lighting is soft diffused sunlight from the upper left, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: Sunlit open ocean, near the surface

option_b.label: Coral Reef: Explore a Tiny Hidden City
option_b.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. A vibrant, bustling coral reef ecosystem with countless tiny, colorful fish darting in and out of intricate coral structures. Soft diffused sunlight from the upper left filters through the clear water, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: Wow, this is like a busy underwater metropolis! Coral reefs are called the 'rainforests of the sea' because they have so much diversity. Did you know corals are actually small animals, not plants? Look, we found a family hiding in a stinging anemone!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. Leo, a girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard, wearing a snorkel mask and flippers, hovers above a vibrant sea anemone where a small clownfish is peeking out, all in clear, sun-dappled warm water. The lighting is soft diffused sunlight from the upper left, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: Every creature here relies on each other. The clownfish protect the anemone, and the coral provides homes for everyone. But these precious reefs are delicate! Changes in water temperature and pollution can hurt the entire ecosystem. We must be careful visitors!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. Leo, a girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard, floats still, observing a complex, branching staghorn coral formation teeming with small blue and yellow fish, in clear, sunlit tropical water. The lighting is soft diffused sunlight from the upper left, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: The ocean is a massive, wondrous kingdom, from the sunny top to the crushing depths. It gives us oxygen, food, and controls our climate. By keeping the water clean and respecting the creatures, we can ensure the ocean stays healthy and magical for everyone!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted watercolor illustration with rich texture, visible brushstrokes, and layered color glazes. Leo, a girl, about 9 years old, with deep warm-brown skin and a head of tight, short curly black hair. She has bright, round amber-brown eyes and a smattering of freckles across her nose and cheeks. She is small and energetic, the shortest explorer in the group. She always wears her signature bright red, waterproof explorer jacket with large pockets and a utility belt, over a striped orange and white long-sleeved shirt, dark green cargo pants, and sturdy brown hiking boots with orange laces. She never goes anywhere without her yellow dive watch and a small, compass on a lanyard, stands on the beach as the sun sets over the ocean, holding a collection of natural seashells, looking out at the calm, colorful water with a sense of peace. The lighting is soft diffused sunlight from the upper left, creating warm golden highlights and cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: Holding shells and watching the sunset
